behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, because correctness is about what the real LLM generates from NL requirements. PASS2-AUDIT: Lower levels were challenged. Unit/Integration can validate a signature-checker, but they cannot reveal model mis-generation; deterministic stubs hide that failure (LLM-DEP=yes). Workflow was considered, but rejected because this behavior is a single model-output quality check, not a multi-step agent chain. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real model on a set of interface-constrained prompts; compile/parse each generated function and assert signature equality with expected params/types/return type. Repeat enough times to expose nondeterministic signature drift.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since failure boundary is orchestrator↔runner/bookkeeping module handoff order and stop condition (MOCK-MISS at module boundary). PASS2-AUDIT (Integration brake): (a) real OS/network/subprocess required? no; in-process fake runner can model pass/fail sequence. (b) depends on model output quality? no. (c) multiple real agentic steps required? no. (d) LLM-governed handoff correctness? no, this is deterministic control flow. Higher levels rejected due behavior text focusing on order/early-return orchestration, not live-model correctness. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic fake generator and fake test runner returning [fail, pass, fail]. Assert attempt order is 1 then 2, attempt 3 is never executed, and returned result corresponds to attempt 2.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Workflow, because this is explicitly a live iterative loop with sequential model-dependent steps (generate→test→refine→retest). PASS2-AUDIT: Agentic alone is insufficient since the asserted capability is success across multiple dependent attempts (MULTI-STEP=yes). Lower levels/stubs would mask the real adaptation behavior. FINAL: Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real end-to-end loop against a known prompt where first attempt commonly fails; require evidence that a later (<=3) attempt passes and that refinement context from prior failure was used between steps.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because failure is in wiring test-runner outputs into prompt-construction module. PASS2-AUDIT (Integration brake): (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? no, synthetic failure objects suffice. (b) real AI output quality needed? no, only prompt content assembly. (c) multi-agentic chain required? no. Agentic rejected by behavior text: it checks inclusion of failure metadata in prompt, not model judgment. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with a fake failing test result payload; capture the constructed refinement prompt and assert it contains each failed test name and corresponding failure message.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Workflow, because this requires at least two sequential real model decisions and a context handoff. PASS2-AUDIT: Agentic was considered, but rejected since single-call agentic testing cannot validate cross-attempt adaptation; behavior text explicitly says “next live attempt” and “in response to prior failure context” (MULTI-STEP=yes). Lower levels with stubs can always force changed output and hide real failure. FINAL: Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to failure, then attempt 2 with real refinement prompt; assert attempt-2 code differs materially from attempt-1 and that changes align with previously failed assertions/errors.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since this is orchestrator control + attempt bookkeeping + error shaping across local modules. PASS2-AUDIT (Integration brake): (a) real OS/network/subprocess required? no, deterministic failing runner can emulate failures. (b) model-output correctness required? no. (c) multiple real agentic steps required? no; loop count and payload integrity are deterministic orchestration concerns. System/Agentic rejected due behavior text focusing on stop condition and structured error content. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic dependencies that fail all attempts; execute orchestration and assert exactly 3 attempts were run, loop terminated, and returned error includes per-attempt code/result metadata for all three failures.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, explicitly non-agentic and stubbed outputs, focused on local module wiring. PASS2-AUDIT (Integration brake): (a) real OS/network/subprocess required? not required by behavior text; can be exercised with in-process test executor abstraction. (b) real AI output required? explicitly no. (c) multi-agentic chain? no. System was considered because some implementations run tests in subprocess, but rejected here since behavior text disqualifies dependence on live infra by specifying deterministic stubbed model outputs and non-agentic wiring validation. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline with deterministic generator stubs and deterministic test-runner outcomes; assert generated code passes through execution and bookkeeping layers with correct attempt state transitions and final status.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Workflow due explicit full live loop with sequential model-dependent steps and success criterion spanning attempts. PASS2-AUDIT: Agentic was considered but rejected because this behavior is inherently cross-step (initial failure then later success). Lower levels cannot catch failures in iterative adaptation with real model behavior. FINAL: Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live pipeline on a curated hard prompt corpus containing at least one case that reliably fails first pass; assert first attempt fails, subsequent refined attempt (within limit) passes, and final artifact satisfies the real test suite.
